issues.title: AWS Bedrock models tools support
issues.body:
Hi team, 
Could you extend the tools support to AWS Bedrock models?

Amazon Bedrock supports tool calling with the following models.
    Anthropic Claude 3 models
    Mistral AI Large
    Cohere Command R and Command R+

AWS Bedrock documentation: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/tool-use.html

Thanks for the great work !!

comments.body:
I'm working on this, but first one is Anthropic Claude 3 models.

Mistral AI models and Cohere Command R models have not been merged yet:
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/808
https://github.com/spring-projects/spring-ai/pull/667
@tzolov plz take a look at recent AWS Bedrock PRs, I wonder if creating a PR includes all supported tool models instead of once a time.
Thanks, this is a large change, so will take a bit to digest.
@tzolov Hi guys! Any updates about Anthropic Claude 3 tools support ?

@piratovi we are working on it PR #1650 
The main parts hopefully will be released with the forthcoming M4 milestone.

Resolved by the [Amazon Bedrock Converse API](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/userguide/conversation-inference.html). 

> Following the Bedrock recommendations, Spring AI is transitioning to using Amazon Bedrock’s Converse API for all chat conversation implementations in Spring AI. While the existing [InvokeModel API](https://docs.spring.io/spring-ai/reference/api/bedrock-chat.html) supports conversation applications, we strongly recommend adopting the Converse API for all Char conversation models.
> The Converse API does not support embedding operations, so these will remain in the current API and the embedding model functionality in the existing InvokeModel API will be maintained

> Bedrock Converse provides a unified interface for conversational AI models with enhanced capabilities including tool calling, multimodal inputs, and streaming responses.
